snippets.title: An update on recent Claude Code quality reports
snippets.takeaway: An update on recent Claude Code quality reports. Over the past month, we’ve been looking into reports that Claude’s responses have worsened for some users.
snippets.summary: An update on recent Claude Code quality reports. Over the past month, we’ve been looking into reports that Claude’s responses have worsened for some users. We’ve traced these reports to three separate changes that affected Claude Code, the Claude Agent SDK, and Claude Cowork. The API was not impacted. All three issues have now been resolved as of April 20 (v2.1.116).

snippets.title: Scaling Managed Agents: Decoupling the brain from the hands
snippets.takeaway: Scaling Managed Agents: Decoupling the brain from the hands. Get started with Claude Managed Agents by following our docs .
snippets.summary: Scaling Managed Agents: Decoupling the brain from the hands. Get started with Claude Managed Agents by following our docs . A running topic on the Engineering Blog is how to build effective agents and design harnesses for long-running work . A common thread across this work is that harnesses encode assumptions about what Claude can’t do on its own. However, those assumptions need to be frequently questioned because they can go stale as models improve.

snippets.title: New in Claude Managed Agents: self-hosted sandboxes and MCP tunnels
snippets.takeaway: New in Claude Managed Agents: self-hosted sandboxes and MCP tunnels. Starting today, Claude Managed Agents can operate in a sandbox you control and connect to your private Model...
snippets.summary: New in Claude Managed Agents: self-hosted sandboxes and MCP tunnels. Starting today, Claude Managed Agents can operate in a sandbox you control and connect to your private Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers. Both the sandbox where an agent executes tools and the services it reaches run within the established boundaries of your enterprise, under your security and runtime controls.

A running topic on the Engineering Blog is how to build effective agents and design harnesses for long-running work . A common thread across this work is that harnesses encode assumptions about what Claude can’t do on its own. However, those assumptions need to be frequently questioned because they can go stale as models improve.

snippets.summary: Scaling Managed Agents: Decoupling the brain from the hands. Get started with Claude Managed Agents by following our docs . A running topic on the Engineering Blog is how to build effective agents and design harnesses for long-running work . A common thread across this work is that harnesses encode assumptions about what Claude can’t do on its own. However, those assumptions need to be frequently questioned because they can go stale as models improve.
